title: Example: Actuarial Estimates (ac.*)
text: SAS source: examples/ac.death.AVC.sas Purpose: Compute Kaplan-Meier survival estimate death AVC repair. nonparametric foundation fitting parametric hazard model. ac.* examples establish baseline nonparametric estimate fitted parametric hazard model compared visually. HAZARD workflow, always run ac.* first characterize raw data, hz.* fit parametric shape. hvtiPlotR::hv_survival() + plot() produces publication-quality KM plots confidence intervals matching SAS PROC LIFETEST PLOTS=(S) output style.
code: PROC LIFETEST DATA=AVCS METHOD=KM PLOTS=(S H) OUTSURV=KMOUT;   TIME INT_DEAD * DEAD(0); RUN; # avcs <- read.table("data/avc", ...)  # Kaplan-Meier using survival package library(survival)  km_fit <- survfit(Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ 1, data = avcs) summary(km_fit)  # Plot with hvtiPlotR hv_obj <- hv_survival(avcs, time_col = "INT_DEAD", event_col = "DEAD") plot(hv_obj, type = "survival") +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Kaplan-Meier: Death after AVC repair"   ) +   hv_theme()

what: bs.death.AVC — Bootstrap CI for AVC death hazard
title: Example: Bootstrap Confidence Intervals (bs.*)
text: SAS source: examples/bs.death.AVC.sas Purpose: Generate bootstrap confidence intervals parametric hazard function fitted AVC death data. bootstrap replicate refits model accumulates prediction curves; CI summarized across replicates.
code: %BOOTSTRAP(   DATA    = AVCS,   HAZEST  = EXAMPLES.HMDEATH,   N       = 200,   SEED    = 12345,   OUT     = BSOUT ); set.seed(3201) n <- 280 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.6),   AGE = rnorm(n, 6, 3),   COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),   MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),   OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),   OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12),   STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.12),   ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1) )  lp <- -2.5 + 0.05 * avcs$AGE + 0.6 * avcs$COM_IV + 0.4 * avcs$MAL +   0.25 * (avcs$STATUS >= 3) + 0.4 * avcs$INC_SURG avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))  X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                           "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  fit_hm <- hazard(   time = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status = avcs$DEAD,   x = X,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, rep(0, ncol(X))),   dist = "weibull",   fit = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 400) )  time_grid <- data.frame(time = seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 40))  # Bootstrap loop (smaller n for vignette runtime). set.seed(12345) n_boot <- 60  boot_curves <- replicate(n_boot, simplify = FALSE, {   idx    <- sample(nrow(avcs), replace = TRUE)   boot_d <- avcs[idx, ]   boot_x <- data.matrix(boot_d[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                                    "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])    out <- tryCatch({     boot_fit <- hazard(       time   = boot_d$INT_DEAD,       status = boot_d$DEAD,       x      = boot_x,       theta  = coef(fit_hm),       dist   = "weibull",       fit    = TRUE,       control = list(maxit = 250)     )      predict(boot_fit, newdata = cbind(time_grid, as.data.frame(boot_x[1:nrow(time_grid), ])),             type = "survival")   }, error = function(e) {     rep(NA_real_, nrow(time_grid))   })    out })  length(boot_curves)

title: Example: Bootstrap Confidence Intervals (bs.*)
text: SAS source: examples/bs.death.AVC.summary.sas Purpose: Compute percentile-based confidence bands bootstrap replicates produce publication-ready hazard function plots shaded CI.
code: ci_bands <- do.call(rbind, boot_curves) ci_bands <- ci_bands[rowSums(is.na(ci_bands)) == 0, , drop = FALSE] median_surv <- apply(ci_bands, 2, median) lower <- apply(ci_bands, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025) upper <- apply(ci_bands, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975)  bootstrap_summary <- data.frame(   time = time_grid$time,   surv_median = median_surv,   surv_lower = lower,   surv_upper = upper )  head(bootstrap_summary)  # Plot parametric survival with bootstrap 95% CI hz_bs <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = data.frame(     time     = bootstrap_summary$time,     survival = bootstrap_summary$surv_median * 100,     lower    = bootstrap_summary$surv_lower * 100,     upper    = bootstrap_summary$surv_upper * 100   ),   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col    = "lower",   upper_col    = "upper" )  plot(hz_bs) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Bootstrap 95% CI — AVC death hazard model"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Getting Started with TemporalHazard
code: new_patients <- data.frame(   time = c(0.5, 1.5, 3.0),   age = c(50, 65, 75),   nyha = c(1, 3, 4),   shock = c(0, 0, 1) )  pred_input <- new_patients  new_patients$linear_predictor <- predict(fit, newdata = pred_input, type = "linear_predictor") new_patients$hazard_multiplier <- predict(fit, newdata = pred_input, type = "hazard") new_patients$survival <- predict(fit, newdata = pred_input, type = "survival") new_patients$cumulative_hazard <- predict(fit, newdata = pred_input, type = "cumulative_hazard")  new_patients #>   time age nyha shock linear_predictor hazard_multiplier  survival #> 1  0.5  50    1     0        0.2420363          1.273840 0.9494097 #> 2  1.5  65    3     0        0.5802020          1.786399 0.7743185 #> 3  3.0  75    4     1        0.7709289          2.161774 0.5046535 #>   cumulative_hazard #> 1        0.05191482 #> 2        0.25577202 #> 3        0.68388317

title: Getting Started with TemporalHazard
text: hvtiPlotR package provides publication-quality plotting parametric hazard models via hv_hazard() plot() method. build survival curve fine time grid median-profile patient overlay Kaplan-Meier nonparametric estimate raw data. Figure 1: Parametric Weibull survival curve Kaplan-Meier overlay
code: if (requireNamespace("hvtiPlotR", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(hvtiPlotR)    # Parametric curve on a fine grid   t_grid <- seq(0.05, max(dat$time), length.out = 80)   curve_df <- data.frame(     time = t_grid,     AGE = median(dat$age),     nyha = 2,     shock = 0   )   curve_df$survival <- predict(fit, newdata = curve_df, type = "survival")    # Kaplan-Meier empirical overlay   km <- survival::survfit(survival::Surv(time, status) ~ 1, data = dat)   km_df <- data.frame(     time = km$time,     estimate = km$surv * 100   )    # Build plot with hv_hazard + plot   hz_obj <- hv_hazard(     curve_data   = transform(curve_df, survival = survival * 100),     x_col        = "time",     estimate_col = "survival",     empirical    = km_df,     emp_x_col    = "time",     emp_estimate_col = "estimate"   )    plot(hz_obj) +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +     ggplot2::labs(       x = "Years after surgery",       y = "Freedom from death (%)",       title = "Parametric survival vs Kaplan-Meier"     ) +     hv_theme_manuscript() }

what: hm.death.AVC — Multivariable model, death after AVC repair
title: Example: Multivariable Hazard Model (hm.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hm.death.AVC.sas Purpose: Final multivariable hazard model death repair atrioventricular septal defects. Covariates split across EARLY (operative hazard) CONSTANT (constant hazard) phases.
code: %HAZARD( PROC HAZARD DATA=AVCS P CONSERVE OUTHAZ=OUTEST CONDITION=14 QUASI;      TIME INT_DEAD;      EVENT DEAD;      PARMS MUE=0.3504743 THALF=0.1905077 NU=1.437416 M=1 FIXM            MUC=4.391673E-07;      EARLY   AGE=-0.03205774, COM_IV=1.336675, MAL=0.6872028,              OPMOS=-0.01963377, OP_AGE=0.0002086689, STATUS=0.5169533;      CONSTANT INC_SURG=1.375285, ORIFICE=3.11765, STATUS=1.054988; ); set.seed(2201) n <- 320 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.6),   AGE = rnorm(n, mean = 6, sd = 3),   COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),   MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.25),   OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),   OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12),   STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.15),   ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1) )  lp <- -2.4 + 0.04 * avcs$AGE + 0.55 * avcs$COM_IV + 0.45 * avcs$MAL +   0.20 * (avcs$STATUS >= 3) + 0.35 * avcs$INC_SURG + 0.50 * avcs$ORIFICE avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))  X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                            "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  fit_hm <- hazard(   time    = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status  = avcs$DEAD,   x       = X,   theta   = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, rep(0, ncol(X))),   dist    = "weibull",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 500) )  fit_hm

what: hm.death.AVC.deciles — Goodness of fit by decile
title: Example: Multivariable Hazard Model (hm.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hm.death.AVC.deciles.sas Purpose: Validate AVC multivariable model comparing observed vs predicted event rates across deciles risk.
code: # Decile-style risk grouping from linear predictor. risk_score <- predict(fit_hm, type = "linear_predictor") q_breaks <- unique(quantile(risk_score, probs = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)) if (length(q_breaks) < 3) {   decile <- cut(rank(risk_score, ties.method = "average"),                 breaks = quantile(rank(risk_score, ties.method = "average"), probs = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)),                 include.lowest = TRUE) } else {   decile <- cut(risk_score, breaks = q_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE) }  aggregate(avcs$DEAD, by = list(decile = decile), FUN = mean)

title: Example: Multivariable Hazard Model (hm.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hm.death.patient.sas Purpose: Patient-level parametric hazard model individual risk scores.
code: new_patients <- data.frame(   time = c(0.5, 1.5, 3.0),   AGE = c(2, 8, 12),   COM_IV = c(0, 1, 1),   MAL = c(0, 0, 1),   OPMOS = c(2, 6, 10),   OP_AGE = c(1, 4, 8),   STATUS = c(1, 3, 4),   INC_SURG = c(0, 0, 1),   ORIFICE = c(0, 1, 1) )  patient_lp <- predict(fit_hm, newdata = new_patients, type = "linear_predictor") patient_surv <- predict(fit_hm, newdata = new_patients, type = "survival")  patient_results <- cbind(new_patients, lp = patient_lp, survival = patient_surv) patient_results  # Plot predicted survival curves for each patient profile t_grid <- seq(0.05, 4.0, length.out = 40) patient_curves <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(new_patients)), function(i) {   nd <- new_patients[rep(i, length(t_grid)), ]   nd$time <- t_grid   data.frame(     time = t_grid,     survival = predict(fit_hm, newdata = nd, type = "survival") * 100,     patient = paste0("Patient ", i)   ) }))  hz_patients <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = patient_curves,   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "patient" )  plot(hz_patients) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Patient-specific predicted survival — AVC model"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Example: Multivariable Hazard Model (hm.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hm.deadp.VALVES.sas Purpose: Multivariable hazard model death valve surgery using VALVES dataset.
code: set.seed(2202) n <- 260 valves <- data.frame(   follow_time = rexp(n, rate = 0.45),   age = rnorm(n, mean = 67, sd = 11),   nyha = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   valve_type = factor(sample(c("bio", "mech"), n, replace = TRUE)) ) valves$dead <- rbinom(   n,   1,   plogis(-2.1 + 0.025 * valves$age + 0.35 * (valves$nyha >= 3) + 0.20 * (valves$valve_type == "mech")) )  X_valves <- model.matrix(~ age + nyha + valve_type, data = valves)[, -1, drop = FALSE]  fit_valves <- hazard(   time    = valves$follow_time,   status  = valves$dead,   x       = X_valves,   theta   = c(mu = 0.20, nu = 1.00, rep(0, ncol(X_valves))),   dist    = "weibull",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 400) )  fit_valves

what: hp.death.AVC — Predicted survival after AVC repair
title: Example: Hazard Prediction (hp.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hp.death.AVC.sas Purpose: Compute plot predicted survival hazard curves fitted AVC model.
code: %HAZPRED( PROC HAZARD DATA=OUTEST IN=AVCS OUT=HPOUT;      TIME INT_DEAD;      PREDICT SURVIVAL HAZARD; ); set.seed(3201) n <- 240 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.55) + 0.05,   DEAD = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.52),   AGE = rnorm(n, mean = 6, sd = 3),   STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2) )  fit_hm <- hazard(   Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ AGE + STATUS + MAL,   data = avcs,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0),   dist = "weibull",   fit = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 300) )  time_grid <- seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 40) base_profile <- data.frame(   time = time_grid,   AGE = median(avcs$AGE),   STATUS = 2,   MAL = 0 )  pred_grid <- transform(   base_profile,   survival = predict(fit_hm, newdata = base_profile, type = "survival"),   cumulative_hazard = predict(fit_hm, newdata = base_profile, type = "cumulative_hazard") )  head(pred_grid)  # Plot predicted survival for median-profile patient km_avc <- survival::survfit(   survival::Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ 1, data = avcs ) km_df <- data.frame(time = km_avc$time, estimate = km_avc$surv * 100)  hz_obj <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = transform(pred_grid, survival = survival * 100),   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   empirical    = km_df,   emp_x_col    = "time",   emp_estimate_col = "estimate" )  plot(hz_obj) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Predicted survival — median-profile AVC patient"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Example: Hazard Prediction (hp.*)
text: SAS sources: examples/hp.death.AVC.hm1.sas, examples/hp.death.AVC.hm2.sas Purpose: Compare predicted hazard curves two fitted multivariable models (e.g., alternative covariate specifications).
code: fit_hm1 <- hazard(   Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ AGE + STATUS,   data = avcs,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0),   dist = "weibull",   fit = TRUE )  fit_hm2 <- hazard(   Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ AGE + STATUS + MAL,   data = avcs,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0),   dist = "weibull",   fit = TRUE )  compare_grid <- data.frame(   time = rep(seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 25), times = 2),   AGE = median(avcs$AGE),   STATUS = 3,   MAL = rep(c(0, 1), each = 25),   model = rep(c("Model1", "Model2"), each = 25) )  compare_grid$survival <- c(   predict(fit_hm1, newdata = subset(compare_grid, model == "Model1")[, c("time", "AGE", "STATUS")], type = "survival"),   predict(fit_hm2, newdata = subset(compare_grid, model == "Model2")[, c("time", "AGE", "STATUS", "MAL")], type = "survival") )  head(compare_grid)

code: scenario_grid <- rbind(   data.frame(profile = "low-risk", time = seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 20), AGE = quantile(avcs$AGE, 0.25), STATUS = 1, MAL = 0),   data.frame(profile = "high-risk", time = seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 20), AGE = quantile(avcs$AGE, 0.75), STATUS = 4, MAL = 1) )  scenario_grid$survival <- predict(   fit_hm,   newdata = scenario_grid[, c("time", "AGE", "STATUS", "MAL")],   type = "survival" ) scenario_grid$hazard_multiplier <- predict(   fit_hm,   newdata = scenario_grid[, c("time", "AGE", "STATUS", "MAL")],   type = "hazard" )  head(scenario_grid)  # Plot low-risk vs high-risk comparison hz_compare <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = transform(scenario_grid, survival = survival * 100),   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "profile" )  plot(hz_compare) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Low-risk vs high-risk predicted survival"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Example: Hazard Prediction (hp.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hp.dthip.PAIVS.time.sas Purpose: Predict patient-specific hazard time PAIVS (Pulmonary Atresia Intact Ventricular Septum) dataset.
code: set.seed(42)  # Synthetic PAIVS-like example for time-varying risk demonstration. n <- 120 paivs <- data.frame(      time = rexp(n, rate = 0.25) + 0.05,      dead = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.65),      vent_score = rnorm(n),      shunt = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.4) )  # Define piecewise windows for β(t): early, mid, late follow-up. tv_windows <- c(1.0, 3.0)  # Weibull with time-varying coefficients via windowed design expansion. # theta layout with two predictors and 3 windows: # [mu, nu, vent_w1, shunt_w1, vent_w2, shunt_w2, vent_w3, shunt_w3] fit_paivs <- hazard(      time = paivs$time,      status = paivs$dead,      x = paivs[, c("vent_score", "shunt")],      time_windows = tv_windows,      theta = c(0.4, 1.1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),      dist = "weibull",      fit = TRUE )  # Time-specific risk summaries. pred_df <- paivs pred_df$lp <- predict(      fit_paivs,      newdata = pred_df[, c("time", "vent_score", "shunt")],      type = "linear_predictor" ) pred_df$haz_mult <- predict(      fit_paivs,      newdata = pred_df[, c("time", "vent_score", "shunt")],      type = "hazard" )  head(pred_df[, c("time", "lp", "haz_mult")])

title: Example: Hazard Sensitivity & Scenarios (hs.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hs.death.AVC.hm1.sas Purpose: Evaluate sensitivity AVC death model covariate changes. Compares predicted survival median vs extreme covariate values.
code: set.seed(4201) n <- 300 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.65),   AGE = rnorm(n, mean = 6, sd = 3),   COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),   MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.25),   OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),   OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12),   STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.12),   ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1) )  lp <- -2.6 + 0.05 * avcs$AGE + 0.65 * avcs$COM_IV + 0.35 * avcs$MAL +   0.22 * (avcs$STATUS >= 3) + 0.45 * avcs$INC_SURG avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))  X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                           "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  fit_hm1 <- hazard(   time = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status = avcs$DEAD,   x = X,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, rep(0, ncol(X))),   dist = "weibull",   fit = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 400) )  # Define reference and scenario covariate profiles ref_profile <- data.frame(   AGE = median(avcs$AGE), COM_IV = 0, MAL = 0,   OPMOS = median(avcs$OPMOS), OP_AGE = median(avcs$OP_AGE),   STATUS = 2, INC_SURG = 0, ORIFICE = 0 )  # Scenario: older patient, higher NYHA, important anomaly scen_profile <- ref_profile scen_profile$AGE    <- quantile(avcs$AGE, 0.9) scen_profile$STATUS <- 4 scen_profile$MAL    <- 1  pred_ref <- cbind(data.frame(time = 1.5), ref_profile) pred_scen <- cbind(data.frame(time = 1.5), scen_profile)  eta_ref  <- predict(fit_hm1, newdata = pred_ref, type = "linear_predictor") eta_scen <- predict(fit_hm1, newdata = pred_scen, type = "linear_predictor") s_ref <- predict(fit_hm1, newdata = pred_ref, type = "survival") s_scen <- predict(fit_hm1, newdata = pred_scen, type = "survival")  data.frame(   profile = c("reference", "scenario"),   linear_predictor = c(eta_ref, eta_scen),   survival_t1_5 = c(s_ref, s_scen) )  # Plot sensitivity: reference vs scenario over time t_grid <- seq(0.05, 4.0, length.out = 40) sens_curves <- rbind(   data.frame(time = t_grid, ref_profile[rep(1, length(t_grid)), ], profile = "Reference"),   data.frame(time = t_grid, scen_profile[rep(1, length(t_grid)), ], profile = "High-risk scenario") )  sens_curves$survival <- predict(   fit_hm1,   newdata = sens_curves[, c("time", "AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS",                             "OP_AGE", "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")],   type = "survival" ) * 100  hz_sens <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = sens_curves,   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "profile" )  plot(hz_sens) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Sensitivity analysis — reference vs high-risk"   ) +   hv_theme()

what: hs.dthar.TGA.setup / hs.dthar.TGA.hs1 — Arrhythmia after TGA repair
title: Example: Hazard Sensitivity & Scenarios (hs.*)
text: SAS sources: examples/hs.dthar.TGA.setup.sas, examples/hs.dthar.TGA.hs1.sas Purpose: Two-step analysis arrhythmia transposition great arteries (TGA) repair. setup file prepares dataset fits hazard model; hs1 runs scenario predictions.
code: set.seed(4202) n <- 260 tga <- data.frame(   time_ar = rexp(n, rate = 0.55),   age = runif(n, 0.5, 12),   repair_type = rbinom(n, 1, 0.45),   bypass_time = rnorm(n, mean = 110, sd = 25) ) tga$arrhythmia <- rbinom(   n,   1,   plogis(-3.0 + 0.06 * tga$age + 0.55 * tga$repair_type + 0.008 * tga$bypass_time) )  tga_x <- data.matrix(tga[, c("age", "repair_type", "bypass_time")])  fit_tga <- hazard(   time    = tga$time_ar,   status  = tga$arrhythmia,   x       = tga_x,   theta   = c(log_lambda = log(0.18), rep(0, ncol(tga_x))),   dist    = "exponential",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 350) )  fit_tga scenarios <- expand.grid(   age         = c(1, 5, 10),   repair_type = c(0, 1),   bypass_time = median(tga$bypass_time),   time = c(1, 3, 5) )  eta_scenarios <- predict(fit_tga, newdata = scenarios, type = "linear_predictor") s_scenarios <- predict(fit_tga, newdata = scenarios, type = "survival")  scenario_results <- cbind(scenarios, linear_predictor = eta_scenarios,                           survival = s_scenarios) scenario_results  # Plot survival by age and repair type across time t_fine <- seq(0.1, 5.0, length.out = 40) scenario_curves <- expand.grid(   time = t_fine,   age = c(1, 5, 10),   repair_type = c(0, 1),   bypass_time = median(tga$bypass_time) ) scenario_curves$group <- paste0(   "Age ", scenario_curves$age,   ifelse(scenario_curves$repair_type == 1, " (repair)", " (no repair)") ) scenario_curves$survival <- predict(   fit_tga, newdata = scenario_curves, type = "survival" ) * 100  hz_tga <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = scenario_curves,   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "group" )  plot(hz_tga) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after TGA repair",     y = "Freedom from arrhythmia (%)",     title = "Scenario analysis — arrhythmia risk by age and repair type"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Example: Estimate Hazard Function (hz.*)
text: SAS source: examples/hz.death.AVC.sas Purpose: Estimate parametric hazard function death repair atrioventricular septal defects. covariates — shape parameters .
code: %HAZARD( PROC HAZARD DATA=AVCS NOCOV NOCOR CONDITION=14;      TIME INT_DEAD;      EVENT DEAD;      PARMS MUE=0.2 THALF=0.15 NU=1.4 M=1; ); # Self-contained synthetic cohort (replace with real AVCS data in production). set.seed(1201) n <- 220 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.7),   DEAD = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.55) )  fit_hz <- hazard(   time    = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status  = avcs$DEAD,   x       = NULL,   theta   = c(mu = 0.30, nu = 1.15),   dist    = "weibull",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 300) )  fit_hz  # Predict survival curve on a follow-up grid. hz_grid <- data.frame(time = seq(0.05, 4.0, length.out = 40)) hz_grid$survival <- predict(fit_hz, newdata = hz_grid, type = "survival")  # Kaplan-Meier overlay km_avc <- survival::survfit(survival::Surv(INT_DEAD, DEAD) ~ 1, data = avcs) km_df <- data.frame(time = km_avc$time, estimate = km_avc$surv * 100)  hz_obj <- hv_hazard(   curve_data   = transform(hz_grid, survival = survival * 100),   x_col        = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   empirical    = km_df,   emp_x_col    = "time",   emp_estimate_col = "estimate" )  plot(hz_obj) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Years after repair",     y = "Freedom from death (%)",     title = "Weibull parametric survival — AVC death"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Example: Estimate Hazard Function (hz.*)
text: SAS sources: examples/hz.te123.OMC.sas, examples/hz.tm123.OMC.sas Purpose: Three-phase hazard model (early, constant, late) OMC (Ontario Multicenter) dataset. te123 uses event-time input; tm123 uses model time-magnitude parameterization.
code: set.seed(1203) n <- 280 omc <- data.frame(   lo = runif(n, min = 0.05, max = 2.0),   width = runif(n, min = 0.05, max = 1.0) ) omc$hi <- omc$lo + omc$width omc$status <- sample(c(1, 0, -1, 2), size = n, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.40, 0.35, 0.10, 0.15)) omc$time <- ifelse(omc$status %in% c(-1, 2), omc$hi, omc$lo)  fit_omc <- hazard(   time    = omc$time,   status  = omc$status,   time_lower = omc$lo,   time_upper = omc$hi,   theta   = c(log_alpha = log(0.20), log_beta = log(1.30)),   dist    = "loglogistic",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 400) )  fit_omc

title: Example: Logistic/Gompertz Exploratory Plots (lg.*)
text: SAS source: examples/lg.death.AVC.sas Purpose: candidate covariate, fit logistic (early hazard) Gompertz (late hazard) model plot estimated relationship. guides covariate transformation decisions (log, power, inverse) hazard model.
code: %LOGIT(   DATA  = AVCS,   EVENT = DEAD,   VARS  = AGE STATUS INC_SURG OPMOS MAL COM_IV ORIFICE OP_AGE ); set.seed(5201) n <- 360 avcs <- data.frame(   INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.6),   AGE = rnorm(n, mean = 6, sd = 3),   STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.15),   OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),   MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.25),   COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),   ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1),   OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12) )  lp <- -2.7 + 0.05 * avcs$AGE + 0.20 * avcs$STATUS + 0.55 * avcs$INC_SURG +   0.03 * avcs$OPMOS + 0.45 * avcs$MAL + 0.60 * avcs$COM_IV +   0.35 * avcs$ORIFICE + 0.01 * avcs$OP_AGE avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))  candidates <- c("AGE", "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "OPMOS",                 "MAL", "COM_IV", "ORIFICE", "OP_AGE")  # Fit logistic model for each candidate covariate logit_fits <- lapply(candidates, function(var) {   fmla <- as.formula(paste("DEAD ~", var))   glm(fmla, data = avcs, family = binomial()) }) names(logit_fits) <- candidates  # Inspect: which covariates show monotone relationships? coef_tables <- lapply(logit_fits, function(f) coef(summary(f))) coef_tables[["AGE"]]  # Plot univariable covariate effects using hvtiPlotR EDA hv_obj <- hv_eda(avcs, x = "AGE", y = "DEAD") plot(hv_obj) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Age at operation (years)",     y = "Death",     title = "Exploratory: AGE vs mortality"   ) +   hv_theme()

title: Migrating from SAS HAZARD to TemporalHazard
text: legacy HAZARD program SAS macro (%HAZARD(...)) wraps compiled C executable. models event-time data using parametric hazard function multiple phases. vignette canonical reference translating SAS HAZARD analysis native R using TemporalHazard. full formal argument mapping table available programmatically: Formal argument map: SAS HAZARD/C → hazard()
code: knitr::kable(   TemporalHazard::hzr_argument_mapping(),   caption = "Formal argument map: SAS HAZARD/C → hazard()",   col.names = c(     "SAS Statement", "Legacy Input", "C Concept",     "R Parameter", "Required", "Expected Type",     "Transform Rule", "Status", "Notes"   ) )

title: Migrating from SAS HAZARD to TemporalHazard
text: Maps hazard() control list: Additional PROC HAZARD options direct R equivalent yet passed ... named arguments stored fit$legacy_args. Maps directly time: Must non-negative numeric vector time unit original analysis (months, AVC example). Missing values permitted. Maps status: TemporalHazard coerces status numeric; logical vectors also accepted. HAZARD convention uses 1 = occurred, 0 = censored/alive last contact. Maps theta (coefficient/parameter vector) control (fix flags): Note: Full SAS PARMS syntax mirrored one--one public API. current package, supply parameter vector directly via theta record fixed-parameter intent control$fix. legacy parity helpers generate SAS-style control text comparing historical binaries. HAZARD, EARLY CONSTANT define phase-specific covariate coefficients. TemporalHazard unified single design matrix x coefficient vector theta M1. Phase assignment formalised M2 multi-phase likelihood implemented. Current convention (M1): combine covariates x supply corresponding starting coefficients theta. Stepwise variable selection implemented TemporalHazard. now, resolve final variable set outside package fit confirmed model directly R.
code: PROC HAZARD DATA=AVCS NOCOV NOCOR CONDITION=14; fit <- hazard(   ...,   control = list(     nocov      = TRUE,   # suppress covariance output     nocor      = TRUE,   # suppress correlation output     condition  = 14      # CONDITION= switch   ) ) TIME INT_DEAD; fit <- hazard(   time   = avcs$INT_DEAD,   ... ) EVENT DEAD; fit <- hazard(   status = avcs$DEAD,   # 1 = event, 0 = censored   ... ) PARMS MUE=0.3504743 THALF=0.1905077 NU=1.437416 M=1 FIXM       MUC=4.391673E-07; fit <- hazard(   theta   = c(MUE = 0.3504743, THALF = 0.1905077, NU = 1.437416,               M = 1,           MUC   = 4.391673e-07),   control = list(     fix = c("M")   # FIXM → freeze M during optimization   ),   ... ) EARLY  AGE=-0.03205774, COM_IV=1.336675, MAL=0.6872028,        OPMOS=-0.01963377, OP_AGE=0.0002086689, STATUS=0.5169533; CONSTANT INC_SURG=1.375285, ORIFICE=3.11765, STATUS=1.054988; # Build design matrix from the AVC data set X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                            "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  # Starting values from SAS EARLY + CONSTANT blocks combined theta_start <- c(   AGE      = -0.03205774,   COM_IV   =  1.336675,   MAL      =  0.6872028,   OPMOS    = -0.01963377,   OP_AGE   =  0.0002086689,   STATUS   =  0.5169533,   # EARLY phase coefficient   INC_SURG =  1.375285,   ORIFICE  =  3.11765 )  fit <- hazard(   time   = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status = avcs$DEAD,   x      = X,   theta  = theta_start,   dist   = "weibull" ) SELECTION SLE=0.2 SLS=0.1;

code: EARLY  AGE=-0.03205774, COM_IV=1.336675, MAL=0.6872028,        OPMOS=-0.01963377, OP_AGE=0.0002086689, STATUS=0.5169533; CONSTANT INC_SURG=1.375285, ORIFICE=3.11765, STATUS=1.054988; # Build design matrix from the AVC data set X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                            "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  # Starting values from SAS EARLY + CONSTANT blocks combined theta_start <- c(   AGE      = -0.03205774,   COM_IV   =  1.336675,   MAL      =  0.6872028,   OPMOS    = -0.01963377,   OP_AGE   =  0.0002086689,   STATUS   =  0.5169533,   # EARLY phase coefficient   INC_SURG =  1.375285,   ORIFICE  =  3.11765 )  fit <- hazard(   time   = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status = avcs$DEAD,   x      = X,   theta  = theta_start,   dist   = "weibull" )

title: Migrating from SAS HAZARD to TemporalHazard
text: mirrors final multivariable model examples/hm.death.AVC.sas reference C repository.
code: %HAZARD( PROC HAZARD DATA=AVCS P CONSERVE OUTHAZ=OUTEST CONDITION=14 QUASI;      TIME INT_DEAD;      EVENT DEAD;      PARMS MUE=0.3504743 THALF=0.1905077 NU=1.437416 M=1 FIXM            MUC=4.391673E-07;      EARLY   AGE=-0.03205774, COM_IV=1.336675,  MAL=0.6872028,              OPMOS=-0.01963377, OP_AGE=0.0002086689, STATUS=0.5169533;      CONSTANT INC_SURG=1.375285, ORIFICE=3.11765, STATUS=1.054988; ); # Assumed: avcs is a data.frame read from the AVC flat file # (same variables as the SAS DATA step)  avcs <- avcs |>   transform(     LN_AGE   = log(AGE),     LN_OPMOS = log(OPMOS),     LN_INC   = ifelse(is.na(INC_SURG), NA, log(INC_SURG + 1)),     LN_NYHA  = log(STATUS)   )  # Replace missing INC_SURG with column mean (mirrors PROC STANDARD REPLACE) avcs$INC_SURG[is.na(avcs$INC_SURG)] <- mean(avcs$INC_SURG, na.rm = TRUE)  X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",                            "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])  fit <- hazard(   time    = avcs$INT_DEAD,   status  = avcs$DEAD,   x       = X,   theta   = c(     # Hazard shape parameters (from PARMS)     MUE   = 0.3504743,     THALF = 0.1905077,     NU    = 1.437416,     M     = 1,     MUC   = 4.391673e-07,     # Covariate coefficients (from EARLY + CONSTANT blocks)     AGE      = -0.03205774,     COM_IV   =  1.336675,     MAL      =  0.6872028,     OPMOS    = -0.01963377,     OP_AGE   =  0.0002086689,     STATUS   =  0.5169533,     INC_SURG =  1.375285,     ORIFICE  =  3.11765   ),   dist    = "weibull",   control = list(     condition = 14,     quasi     = TRUE,     conserve  = TRUE,     fix       = c("M")   # FIXM   ) )  fit

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: formula Optional formula form Surv(time, status) ~ predictors. provided, overrides direct time/status/x arguments extracts data. Example: hazard(Surv(time, status) ~ x1 + x2, data = df, dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE). data Optional data frame containing variables referenced formula. time Numeric follow-time vector. status Numeric logical event indicator vector. time_lower Optional numeric lower bound vector censoring intervals. Used status == 2 (interval-censored); defaults time NULL. time_upper Optional numeric upper bound vector censoring intervals. Used status %% c(-1, 2); defaults time NULL. x Optional design matrix (data frame coercible matrix). time_windows Optional numeric vector strictly positive cut points piecewise time-varying coefficients. provided, predictor column x expanded one column per time window window gets coefficient. theta Optional numeric coefficient vector (starting values optimization). dist Character baseline distribution label (default "weibull"). fit Logical; TRUE theta provided, fit model via ML (default TRUE). control Named list control options (see Details). ... Additional named arguments retained parity legacy calling conventions.

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: Control parameters: maxit: Maximum iterations (default 1000) reltol: Relative parameter change tolerance (default 1e-5) abstol: Absolute gradient norm tolerance (default 1e-6) method: Optimization method: "bfgs" "nm" (default "bfgs") condition: Condition number control (default 14) nocov, nocor: Suppress covariance/correlation output (legacy; -op M2) Censoring status coding: 1: Exact event time 0: Right-censored time -1: Left-censored upper bound time_upper \(time\) 2: Interval-censored interval \(time_lower, time_upper\) Time-varying coefficients: time_windows supplied, predictors expanded piecewise window interactions window coefficient vector. implemented design-matrix expansion, existing likelihood engines remain unchanged.

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
code: # Fit a Weibull hazard model set.seed(1) time <- rexp(50, rate = 0.3) status <- sample(0:1, 50, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.7)) fit <- hazard(time = time, status = status,               theta = c(0.3, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE) print(fit) #> hazard object #>   observations: 50  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         weibull  #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   log-lik:      -89.5173  #>   converged:    TRUE   # Formula interface df <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, x1 = rnorm(50)) fit2 <- hazard(survival::Surv(time, status) ~ x1, data = df,                theta = c(0.3, 1.0, 0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE)
